types.name: general
types.purpose: General-purpose list where each item is a single text note.

types.name: todos
types.purpose: Task tracking list with due date and workflow status.

fields.name: due
fields.description: Due Date (and optionally time) ideally in parseable ISO 8601 format

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current status of the task such as pending, in-progress, or completed

types.name: people
types.purpose: People/contact list with identity, relationship, and notes.

fields.name: relation
fields.description: How this person relates to you

types.name: habits
types.purpose: Recurring habit tracker with cadence and progress context.

fields.name: frequency
fields.description: Cadence such as daily or weekly

fields.name: progress
fields.description: Current status or progress towards the target. This could be a simple note, a percentage, a measurement, or a count depending on the habit.

fields.name: last_completed
fields.description: Last completion timestamp, ideally in parseable ISO 8601 format

types.name: shopping-items
types.purpose: Shopping planning with quantity, category, and budget context.

fields.name: category
fields.description: Category such as produce or household

fields.name: status
fields.description: Shopping status such as needed, purchased, or out-of-stock

types.name: health-log
types.purpose: Structured health metric logging for trend and routine tracking.

fields.name: recorded_at
fields.description: Measurement timestamp, ideally in parseable ISO 8601 format

fields.name: context
fields.description: Measurement context such as before/after meal or workout

types.name: waiting-on
types.purpose: Track delegated or pending responses with follow-up dates.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: Who owes the response or action

fields.name: requested_at
fields.description: When the request was made, ideally in parseable ISO 8601 format

fields.name: due_by
fields.description: Expected completion date, ideally in parseable ISO 8601 format

fields.name: next_follow_up
fields.description: Planned follow-up timestamp, ideally in parseable ISO 8601 format
